title: rosa woodsii subsp. manca
intro: Rosa woodsii subsp. manca, also known as the Wood's rose or western wood's rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family. This perennial shrub is native to the western United States and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots are used to make herbal preparations that offer various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and treating digestive issues and skin conditions. Traditional herbal preparations of Rosa woodsii subsp. manca include teas, infusions, and salves that can be made by infusing the plant's parts in water or oil.
